outcomes: Identify fundamental and derived SI units and systems of measurement.
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships.

learning_outcomes.topic: Kinematics in One and Two Dimensions
outcomes: Analyze uniform and non-uniform motion using differentiation and integration concepts.
Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs to determine displacement and acceleration.

learning_outcomes.topic: Kinematics in One and Two Dimensions
outcomes: Resolve vectors into rectangular components and perform vector operations (addition, subtraction, dot/cross products).
Derive equations for projectile motion and analyze cases of uniform circular motion.
Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and impulse in relation to motion changes.
Apply Newton's three laws of motion to solve equilibrium and dynamic problems involving concurrent forces.
Analyze static and kinetic friction, rolling friction, and lubrication effects.
Calculate centripetal force required for uniform circular motion in various contexts (level roads, banked curves).
Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using graphical and integral methods.
Apply the Work-Energy Theorem to relate net work to changes in kinetic energy.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze potential energy concepts (including springs).
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions conserving momentum and energy.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
learning_outcomes.topic: Rotational Dynamics and Rigid Body Motion
outcomes: Determine the center of mass for particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rods).
Apply conservation of linear and angular momentum to solve collision and interaction problems.
Calculate torque and analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies under concurrent forces.
Compare linear and rotational motion variables, calculate moment of inertia for simple geometries.
Apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion to describe orbital trajectories.
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy, escape speed, and orbital velocity for satellites.

learning_outcomes.topic: Mechanical Properties of Solids and Fluids
outcomes: Apply Hooke's Law to analyze elastic behavior, Young's modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio.
Calculate hydrostatic pressure variations and apply Pascal's law in hydraulic systems.

learning_outcomes.topic: Mechanical Properties of Solids and Fluids
outcomes: Analyze fluid dynamics including viscosity (Stokes' law), terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow regimes.
Apply Bernoulli's theorem to explain dynamic lift and Torricelli's law.
Analyze modes of heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation) and Blackbody radiation concepts.
Apply the Zeroth Law to define temperature equilibrium.
Use First and Second Laws of Thermodynamics to analyze isothermal, adiabatic, reversible, irreversible, and cyclic processes.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases
outcomes: Derive the equation of state for a perfect gas from kinetic theory assumptions.
Interpret temperature as an average measure of molecular kinetic energy (rms speed).
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases.
Describe periodic functions and derive equations for Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M).
Analyze the dynamics of loaded springs and simple pendulums including time period derivations.
Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and apply the principle of superposition.
Apply Coulomb's Law and the superposition principle to calculate forces between point charges.
Use Gauss's Theorem to determine electric fields for symmetric charge distributions (wire, plane, shell).
Calculate electric potential and energy for various configurations including dipoles.
Analyze the effect of dielectrics on electric fields and capacitor performance (capacitance increase).
Calculate equivalent capacitance for complex networks in series and parallel.
Relate electric current to microscopic parameters (drift velocity, mobility).
Analyze V-I characteristics for linear and non-linear components.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Apply the Biot-Savart Law to calculate magnetic fields from current loops.
Use Ampere's Circuital Law for infinite wires and solenoids.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Distinguish between para-, dia-, and ferro-magnetic substances based on their properties.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Apply Faraday's Law and Lenz's Law to determine induced EMF and current direction.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Analyze phasor diagrams for series LCR circuits to determine phase differences.
Explain the generation of EM waves via changing electric/magnetic fields.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
outcomes: Apply mirror formulae and lens maker's formula to solve image formation problems.
Apply the concept of superposition to explain constructive and destructive interference.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
outcomes: Explain the experimental evidence for the particle nature of light (photoelectric effect).
Describe the Bohr model of the atom including quantization of orbits.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
outcomes: Analyze the I-V characteristics of a semiconductor diode in forward and reverse bias.
